title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Help Version Safety symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: githooks Manual — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A command from a current web page behaves identically on every installed Git version.

stimulus.title: Help Version Safety failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A command from a current web page behaves identically on every installed Git version.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Snapshots Graph evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Snapshots Graph assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 6.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Snapshots Graph has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Snapshots Graph precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Help Version Safety: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 7.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

hints: Name the Help Version Safety precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
